column1: GN[Tournament Game: Meijin Handicap Title Tournament 2014 (2494) R:2 (ebe vs Thorn)]
GN[Ladder Challenge: Thorn(#626) vs Ilka Matin(#457)]
Ilka Matin: you win, thanks for the game
C[Thorn: usually start in the corners
Thorn: usually around the small dark circle
C[Thorn: Then you approach like this
C[Thorn: which is basically just finding an even position
Thorn: and there are many responses to the approach
Thorn: the most common being this one
C[foulmal: so im trying to block off areas of blank space?
C[Thorn: there is a website I use called eidogo.com
Thorn: it shows you the responses you can use in many given situations
C[foulmal: oh word ill look it up
C[Thorn: you don't need to worry about that side
Thorn: that's actually what we call an empty triangle
Thorn: it's a waste of space, you want to be flexible
Thorn: but r17 wasn't joseki either haha
C[foulmal: haha i might just start mimicking you
foulmal: so are we trying for joseki
foulmal: we're trying to like balance the board?
C[Thorn: You have to find a balance of influence and territory
C[Thorn: territory is usually associated with being on the third line
C[foulmal: aiya i wish i hadnt put that black piece right in the middle
Thorn: 4th line is associated with influence
Thorn: C, 3, R, and 17 are all territory related
Thorn: and 4, d, q and 16 are all more territory related
Thorn: There are a lot of terms that I might throw at you. Most of them won't stick, but you'll learn them slowly
Thorn: especially once you see them
foulmal: i get the 4tha nd 3rd thing but joseski im like 8(
Thorn: It's about dividing the corner most of the time
Thorn: the other half it's about getting influence
C[Thorn: Sometimes you can decide between influence and territory with joseki
C[foulmal: and how do you caputre
Thorn: There things called liberties, which is a fancy term for all the spaces that surround your pieces
Thorn: essentially if you place one piece with no pieces around it, it has 4 liberties
Thorn: if you surround these 4 liberties the stone is captured
Thorn: but it gets more complicated as you attach stones to one another
Thorn: because two stones together are 6 liberties
Thorn: also, there is something called having eyes
C[Thorn: which is a little too complicated to explain yet
foulmal: okay for the sake of example can you capture one of my pieces
C[Thorn: you'll caputre mine as well haha
Thorn: or one eye I should say
foulmal: and you lose a point at the end for putting a piece on top of an eye right
C[Thorn: if it's an eye like mine h11, then you can't place a stone there
Thorn: but i gain a point for taking a stone
C[Thorn: and you gained two for taking mine
C[Thorn: oh god sorry, I wasn't looking
foulmal: dude no worries im trying to translate this web article
Thorn: I was switching between things, forgot I only had 30 seconds
foulmal: i mean you won technically right
Thorn: Alright I gotta focus on homework now. I can teach you more tomorrow in the library if you want
foulmal: haha that depends on how far i get on this hw..but possibly!!
